hero.headline: Paid family and sick leave in Maryland — what employers owe right now
hero.subhead: Two laws, two timelines. Here's what's live and what's coming.

partner_badge.text: Served by Teamed partner network: Suna Solutions, Inc.

answer_cite_capsule.text: Maryland has two paid leave laws running on different timelines. The Healthy Working Families Act is live now — 1 hour of paid sick leave per 30 hours worked, up to 40 hours a year, for employers with 15 or more employees. The FAMLI paid family leave programme starts contributions on 1st January 2027 and pays benefits from 3rd January 2028 at up to 90% of average weekly wages, capped at $1,000/week. Both laws stack on top of federal FMLA.

famli_overview.question: What is Maryland FAMLI and when does it start?
famli_overview.answer_first: Maryland FAMLI is a state-run paid leave programme. Employer contributions begin 1st January 2027; employees can claim benefits from 3rd January 2028.
famli_overview.body:
The Time to Care Act of 2022 created Maryland's Family and Medical Leave Insurance programme. After several legislative delays, the programme reached final regulations in March 2026 (COMAR 09.42) and is now on track. Employers begin payroll deductions and employer contributions on 1st January 2027. Benefits open for claims no later than 3rd January 2028.

Eligible employees get up to 12 weeks of paid leave per qualifying reason — with a possible additional 12 weeks for a separate qualifying event in the same application year, allowing up to 24 weeks total. Wage replacement runs at 90% of average weekly wages for earnings at or below 65% of the Maryland State Average Weekly Wage, and 50% above that threshold. Benefits are capped at $1,000 per week (subject to inflation adjustment).

Qualifying reasons include bonding with a new child (birth, adoption, or foster placement), a serious personal health condition, caring for a family member with a serious health condition, and qualifying military exigency. Your payroll team needs to model the 0.9% contribution into your 2027 cost projections now.

famli_contributions.question: How much does Maryland FAMLI cost employers?
famli_contributions.answer_first: The total FAMLI contribution rate is 0.9% of covered wages up to the Social Security wage cap, split equally: 0.45% employer, 0.45% employee.
famli_contributions.body:
Contributions apply to wages paid from 1st January 2027 through 31st December 2027. The state will announce each subsequent year's rate every November. The statutory ceiling is 1.2% total — the rate cannot exceed that cap regardless of programme costs.

Small employers with fewer than 15 employees (counting both Maryland and out-of-state workers under the same EIN) owe only the employee share of 0.45%. The employer half is waived, but the employee portion is still withheld and remitted. All employees are covered regardless of employer size.

The first remittance is due 30th April 2027 (for Q1 2027 wages). Employers may substitute an approved private plan meeting or exceeding FAMLI benefits — private plan applications open summer 2027. Teamed's payroll team handles contribution setup, withholding, and quarterly remittance through Suna Solutions. Every line is itemised on the invoice; Maryland FAMLI is passed through at cost with no mark-up.

hwfa_sick_leave.question: How much sick leave must Maryland employers provide right now?
hwfa_sick_leave.answer_first: Employers with 15 or more employees must provide paid earned sick and safe leave at 1 hour per 30 hours worked, up to 40 hours a year. Smaller employers must provide unpaid leave on the same terms.
hwfa_sick_leave.body:
The Healthy Working Families Act has been in effect since February 2018. Leave accrues at 1 hour for every 30 hours worked, capped at 40 hours per year. Unused leave carries over up to 40 hours at year end (total accrued balance cannot exceed 64 hours). Employers who front-load the full 40 hours at the start of the year may eliminate carryover tracking — a useful simplification for most payroll setups.

Employees cannot use accrued leave until after the first 106 calendar days of employment — a detail that catches many US hiring managers off guard. Once that window passes, leave is available for illness, injury, preventative care, family member illness, domestic violence matters, and maternity or paternity purposes. Notice requirements: foreseeable leave requires reasonable advance notice of up to 7 days; unforeseeable leave must be flagged as soon as practicable.

Paid leave must be at the employee's normal wage rate. A real human on your country specialist team reviews each Maryland onboarding to confirm the 15-employee threshold, the front-load election, and the 106-day window are reflected in your offer letter.

stacking_fmla.question: How do Maryland paid leave laws interact with federal FMLA?
stacking_fmla.answer_first: Maryland FAMLI and HWFA leave runs concurrently with federal FMLA for qualifying events. You run three separate compliance tracks, not a single combined entitlement.
stacking_fmla.body:
Federal Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) applies to employers with 50 or more employees and provides up to 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected leave. Maryland FAMLI will provide the wage-replacement layer on top of that job protection once benefits open in 2028. The two programmes run concurrently for qualifying FMLA events — the employee doesn't get 12 FMLA weeks plus 12 FAMLI weeks sequentially.

Maryland Healthy Working Families Act sick leave is a separate pot. Employees use HWFA leave for shorter illness or care events; FMLA governs the extended job protection for serious conditions. HWFA leave and FMLA leave may run concurrently when the qualifying reason overlaps.

For employers using Teamed's EOR service through Suna Solutions, you get a single platform tracking all three obligations — FMLA eligibility, HWFA accrual balance, and FAMLI contribution readiness — rather than managing three separate compliance feeds. That's the operational advantage of one workflow from first hire to entity graduation.

payroll_today.question: What do Maryland employers need to do before FAMLI starts?
payroll_today.answer_first: Model the 0.9% FAMLI contribution into 2027 cost projections now. Update HWFA policies if you're still running a 'use-it-or-lose-it' structure that violates the 40-hour carryover rule.
payroll_today.body:
Three actions before 1st January 2027. First, add the FAMLI employer contribution (0.45% × covered wages) to your per-head cost model for any Maryland hire. On a $75,000 salary, that's roughly $338/year in employer cost — modest, but it compounds across a team. Second, confirm your payroll provider can handle FAMLI withholding from day one of 2027 contributions; the first remittance deadline is 30th April 2027. Third, review your HWFA policy for the 106-day waiting period and front-load election — both are frequently misconfigured in standard US HR templates.

Teamed's $599/month flat fee covers the EOR management layer. Maryland statutory costs (FAMLI once live, UI at 2.60% on the first $8,500, and state income tax up to 6.50%) are passed through at cost, itemised on every invoice. No mark-up, no bundling. You see every line before you approve it.

good_to_know.question: What should you know about Maryland's paid leave landscape?
good_to_know.body: Maryland sits in the middle of the US paid leave pack. FAMLI's 0.9% rate is lower than Oregon (1%) and comparable to Massachusetts (0.88%), and the $1,000/week benefit cap is generous relative to most state programmes. The Time to Care Act passed in 2022 but experienced two rounds of legislative delay before reaching final regulations in March 2026 — a pattern worth noting when building compliance timelines. For employers with teams spread across US states, Maryland's stacked HWFA-plus-FAMLI structure mirrors the California model more than the Texas model. Factoring in a FAMLI private plan option (applications open summer 2027) may deliver cost savings at scale if your workforce skews low-claim.

faq_schema.q: Does Maryland have paid family leave in 2026?
faq_schema.a: Not yet. Maryland's FAMLI programme starts employer contributions on 1st January 2027 and opens benefit claims from 3rd January 2028. The programme provides up to 12 weeks of paid leave at up to 90% of average weekly wages, capped at $1,000/week.

faq_schema.q: How much sick leave do Maryland employers have to provide?
faq_schema.a: Employers with 15 or more employees must provide paid earned sick and safe leave at 1 hour per 30 hours worked, up to 40 hours per year. Employees cannot use accrued leave until after 106 calendar days of employment.

faq_schema.q: What is the Maryland FAMLI contribution rate?
faq_schema.a: The total contribution rate is 0.9% of covered wages up to the Social Security wage base, split equally at 0.45% employer and 0.45% employee. Employers with fewer than 15 employees pay only the employee half. Contributions begin 1st January 2027.

faq_schema.q: Can Maryland employees use HWFA sick leave immediately?
faq_schema.a: No. Employees must complete 106 calendar days of employment before using accrued Healthy Working Families Act leave. Leave accrues from day one, but use is restricted during the first 106 days.
